$comment: Lab Map - VLAN Segmentation Example. Fictional VLAN layout for Meridian Systems. Six VLANs with strict segmentation: management gear isolated on VLAN 1, production services on VLAN 10, storage traffic on VLAN 20, staging fully isolated on VLAN 30, workstations on VLAN 40, guest Wi-Fi on VLAN 50. Inter-VLAN rules enforced by OPNsense firewall.

meta.name: Management
interVlanRules: Allow VLAN 10 → VLAN 1: IPMI/iDRAC (UDP 623, TCP 443)
Allow VLAN 40 → VLAN 1: HTTPS (443) — switch management UI only
Deny all other inter-VLAN traffic into VLAN 1
meta.note: Management VLAN — network gear and out-of-band access only. iDRAC9 addresses for srv-compute-01 (10.0.1.11), srv-compute-02 (10.0.1.12), srv-compute-03 IPMI (10.0.1.13), nas-01 IPMI (10.0.1.14).

meta.name: Production
interVlanRules: Allow VLAN 40 → VLAN 10: HTTPS (443), HTTP (80), DNS (UDP 53), SSH (22)
Allow VLAN 10 → VLAN 20: NFS (2049), S3 (9000), SMB (445)
Allow VPN 10.99.0.0/24 → VLAN 10: all services
Deny VLAN 30 → VLAN 10: all traffic (staging isolation)
meta.note: Production VLAN — all live services. Traefik VIP (10.0.10.2) handles inbound HTTPS. CoreDNS VIP (10.0.10.3) serves as primary resolver for all VLANs.

meta.name: Storage
interVlanRules: Allow VLAN 10 → VLAN 20: NFS (2049), S3 API (9000), HTTPS (443)
Allow VLAN 40 → VLAN 20: SMB (445) — workstation file shares
Deny VLAN 30 → VLAN 20: all traffic
Deny VLAN 50 → VLAN 20: all traffic
meta.note: Dedicated storage VLAN — all NFS, SMB, and S3 traffic isolated here. Static addressing only; no DHCP. Servers on VLAN 10 reach NAS via inter-VLAN routing through edge-router.

meta.name: Staging
interVlanRules: Allow VLAN 30 → Internet: HTTP (80), HTTPS (443) — package downloads
Allow VLAN 30 → VLAN 10: TCP 22 (SSH to GitLab only, IP-restricted)
Deny VLAN 30 → VLAN 10: all other traffic
Deny VLAN 30 → VLAN 20: all traffic
Deny VLAN 30 → VLAN 40: all traffic
meta.note: Isolated staging VLAN — srv-compute-03 runs GitLab CI runners here. Cannot reach production services. Mirrors production Docker images but uses separate data volumes.

meta.name: Workstations
interVlanRules: Allow VLAN 40 → VLAN 10: HTTPS (443), HTTP (80), SSH (22), DNS (UDP 53)
Allow VLAN 40 → VLAN 20: SMB (445), NFS (2049) — file shares
Allow VLAN 40 → VLAN 1: HTTPS (443) — switch management only
Deny VLAN 40 → VLAN 30: all traffic
Deny VLAN 40 → VLAN 50: all traffic
meta.note: Staff workstation VLAN — Alice (10.0.40.21) and Bob (10.0.40.22) have wired connections. Wireless devices join via office-gw (GL.iNet Flint 2). DHCP served by edge-router.

meta.name: Guest Wi-Fi
interVlanRules: Allow VLAN 50 → Internet: HTTP (80), HTTPS (443) — internet only
Allow VLAN 50 → VLAN 10: DNS (UDP 53) — CoreDNS VIP only
Deny VLAN 50 → VLAN 10: all other traffic
Deny VLAN 50 → VLAN 20: all traffic
Deny VLAN 50 → VLAN 30: all traffic
Deny VLAN 50 → VLAN 40: all traffic
meta.note: Guest network — internet access only with captive portal. Captive portal splash page served by edge-router (OPNsense). No inter-VLAN access except outbound DNS.
OPNsense 24.1 — layer-3 gateway for all VLANs. Manages all inter-VLAN firewall rules and NAT. WireGuard VPN endpoint on 10.99.0.1.
Cisco SG350-28P — all VLANs trunked through but management address is VLAN 1 only.
Cisco SG350-10 — management on VLAN 1. Downlink ports assigned to VLAN 40 (workstations) or VLAN 50 (guest).
VLAN trunk: VLAN 1 (management/iDRAC), VLAN 10 (production services), VLAN 20 (storage — not mounted directly, only via VIP routing).
VLAN trunk: VLAN 1 (management/iDRAC), VLAN 10 (production), VLAN 20 (NFS mount from nas-01 for Nextcloud data).
Isolated to VLAN 30 (staging) plus VLAN 1 management/IPMI. Cannot communicate with production VLAN 10 — firewall deny-all between VLAN 30 and VLAN 10.
VLAN 20 (storage traffic) + VLAN 1 (IPMI management). All NFS and SMB connections routed via VLAN 20.
Docker macvlan container — dedicated IP 10.0.10.2 on VLAN 10. Handles all inbound proxy routing.
Docker macvlan container — dedicated IP 10.0.10.3 on VLAN 10. Primary DNS for all VLANs; pushed via DHCP on every VLAN.
Wired VLAN 40. Accesses git.meridian.io, files.meridian.io, and SMB shares on nas-01.
Wired VLAN 40. Manages infrastructure via Ansible/Terraform. Has SSH access to all servers via bastion policy.
GL.iNet Flint 2 — trunk port with VLAN 40 for staff and VLAN 50 for guest Wi-Fi SSIDs. Separate SSIDs: 'Meridian-Staff' (VLAN 40), 'Meridian-Guest' (VLAN 50).

edges.label: Isolated
edges.note: Staging is fully isolated from production — firewall deny-all rule. CI runners on VLAN 30 cannot reach live services on VLAN 10.

meta.denied: All traffic denied — staging isolation policy

edges.label: Blocked
edges.note: Guest VLAN has internet access only — no access to any internal VLANs except CoreDNS for resolution.

meta.denied: All other traffic denied — internet-only policy
